text.title: Go Standard Layout — Directory Structure Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Standard Layout — Directory Structure application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Top-level dirs MUST be `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/`, `migrations/`; no other top-level dirs without ADR.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go community layout (golang-standards repo + critiques). Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Each binary MUST live under `cmd/<binary-name>/main.go`; no shared main.go.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go community layout. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Application packages MUST live under `internal/<feature>` to enforce non-importability.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go specification. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: `pkg/` MUST hold only stable, externally-reusable libraries; nothing app-specific.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go community layout. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Migration files MUST follow `NNN_<verb>_<noun>.up.sql` + `.down.sql`; numbering monotonic.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: golang-migrate docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.
Rule rooted in source: Go community critiques. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Test fixtures MUST live under `testdata/` (the Go-toolchain-special dir name).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go testing docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
